surface.name: Browser JS Quick Start snippet + first occurrence
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Send the first browser occurrence. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: The Browser JavaScript quick-start snippet reports a test occurrence to the new Rollbar project.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Rollbar setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Browser JavaScript quick-start snippet reports a test occurrence to the new Rollbar project.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The occurrence appears in the Rollbar project dashboard with the test message and browser context.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Send the first browser occurrence.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Browser JS Quick Start snippet + first occurrence surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A browser page where the Browser JS snippet can run.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Rollbar and create a Browser JavaScript project.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.output: The project dashboard and client token are accessible.
primary_path.success_signal: The project dashboard and client token are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Add the documented Browser JS quick-start snippet to a page with the project access token.
primary_path.output: Rollbar is initialized when the page loads.
primary_path.success_signal: Rollbar is initialized when the page loads.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Load the page and invoke the documented test log or error call.
primary_path.output: The browser sends an occurrence to Rollbar.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser sends an occurrence to Rollbar.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Open the Rollbar project and confirm the first occurrence appears.
primary_path.output: The test occurrence is visible in the dashboard.
primary_path.success_signal: The test occurrence is visible in the dashboard.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: A client-side token configured for browser reporting is required.
Browser content-security or network controls can block the reporting request.

sources.title: Send the first browser occurrence
evidence_supported: The Browser JavaScript quick-start snippet reports a test occurrence to the new Rollbar project.
The occurrence appears in the Rollbar project dashboard with the test message and browser context.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Send the first browser occurrence path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
